message:
fix(mux): prevent subscription backpressure deadlocks on Ended/Gap signals

When the downstream consumer stopped draining the mpsc channel, the
poller would block forever on send().await trying to emit Ended or Gap
signals, preventing cancellation from completing and leaking the
connection.

Changes:
- Extract pane_delta_try_emit_ended() helper using try_send (non-blocking)
  instead of the await-based send for PaneDelta::Ended signals
- Convert 4 call sites from blocking pane_delta_send().await to
  non-blocking pane_delta_try_emit_ended()
- Change Gap signal emission from blocking pane_delta_send().await to
  pane_delta_try_send() (fire-and-forget)

New tests:
- concurrent_subscriptions_do_not_cross_talk: verifies two subscriptions
  on different panes stay isolated
- subscription_cancel_closes_connection_when_channel_full: verifies
  cancellation works even when the mpsc channel is full and nobody drains
- subscription_cancel_closes_connection_when_seq_gap_emit_is_backpressured:
  same but for the gap-emission code path

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
